skill.name: scholar-evaluation
skill.description: Evaluate scholarly work with the ScholarEval framework across research questions, literature review, methodology, analysis, results, writing, and citations.
skill.summary: Structured academic quality assessment with scoring rubrics and actionable revision guidance.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but contextual review found no malicious intent, credential access, network exfiltration, arbitrary command execution, or prompt injection. Most high weak-cryptography and reconnaissance hits are false positives from academic rubric language, example command blocks, names, and URLs. Residual risk is low because the helper script reads user-provided JSON paths and can write reports to user-provided output paths.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Read and Write
low_findings.description: The helper script accepts user-supplied paths for score input, optional weights input, and report output. This is expected for a local score calculator, but users should avoid running it on untrusted paths or sensitive files because it can read JSON files and overwrite an output path selected by the user.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly opens user-provided Path arguments for reading and optional writing. The behavior is local and expected, so severity is low rather than high.

low_findings.title: Documentation References External Services and Links
low_findings.description: The skill documentation links to arXiv and suggests K-Dense Web for complex workflows. These are not automatic network calls, but marketplace users should know the skill includes external references and a hosted platform suggestion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation, but there is no code that automatically sends data to them. This is a disclosure issue, not evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Example Command References Missing Schematic Script
low_findings.description: The documentation shows a command for scripts/generate_schematic.py, but that file is not present in the scanned file structure. This could confuse agents or users into invoking unavailable tooling, although it does not create command injection by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command block is explicit and the provided file structure contains only scripts/calculate_scores.py. The risk is operational and low because no dynamic shell execution is implemented.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Findings Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations occur in academic rubric text, score thresholds, author names, or quality-level labels. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, hashing for security, cipher selection, password storage, or token handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary prose and score labels at representative flagged locations. There is no cryptographic API usage in the reviewed script or documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The reported reconnaissance locations are rubric language about evaluation scope, source quality, writing flow, and using the framework. No evidence found of system probing, port scanning, network discovery, or host enumeration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms appear in academic assessment instructions. The files contain no commands or code paths for reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis combined filesystem, command examples, URLs, and a certificate/key keyword hit into a critical heuristic. Context does not confirm credential access, automatic network use, obfuscation, or code execution beyond a normal local Python CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged areas were reviewed and matched benign score calculation, text formatting, and documentation links. No semantic evidence supports malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Research feedback is often inconsistent across reviewers and evaluation stages. This skill applies a structured ScholarEval rubric to assess quality, score dimensions, and prioritize revisions.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates research questions, literature review, methodology, data, analysis, results, writing, and citations.
Applies a five-point rubric with dimension-specific quality indicators.
Produces strengths, weaknesses, critical issues, and prioritized recommendations.
Adapts standards for papers, proposals, literature reviews, theses, abstracts, and venue expectations.
Uses a local Python script to calculate weighted aggregate scores from dimension ratings.
Supports comparative or targeted reviews when users specify an evaluation scope.
limitations: It does not replace expert review by a qualified domain specialist.
The score calculator expects properly formatted local JSON input.
Some rubric dimensions may not apply to theoretical or early-stage work.
The schematic generation command references tooling that is not included in this skill package.

use_cases.title: Review a Manuscript Before Submission
use_cases.description: Assess a draft across quality dimensions and identify high-impact revisions before journal or conference submission.

use_cases.title: Grade Research Proposals Consistently
use_cases.description: Apply a transparent rubric to proposals and provide comparable feedback across students or project teams.

use_cases.title: Improve a Literature Review
use_cases.description: Check coverage, synthesis, source quality, gap identification, and citation balance in a review section.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Paper Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a concise assessment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this research paper draft using the ScholarEval framework. Give an overall assessment, three strengths, three weaknesses, and priority revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Methods Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of research design and analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review only the methodology, data collection, and analysis sections. Score each dimension from 1 to 5 and explain the evidence for each score.

prompt_templates.title: Publication Readiness Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this manuscript for publication readiness for a peer-reviewed journal. Include dimension scores, blocking issues, and a ranked revision plan.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Proposal Evaluation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced comparison across multiple research proposals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these three research proposals using ScholarEval. Score each proposal, explain tradeoffs by dimension, and recommend which proposal should advance.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a review of a draft empirical research paper.
output_examples.output: A structured report with dimension scores, a quality level, key strengths, critical weaknesses, and prioritized revision actions.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether a literature review section is strong enough.
output_examples.output: A focused assessment of coverage, synthesis, source quality, gap identification, and citation balance with concrete improvement steps.

output_examples.input: A user provides dimension scores and asks for an aggregate rating.
output_examples.output: A weighted score summary with quality category, strongest dimensions, weakest dimensions, and recommendation text.

best_practices: Provide the work type, target venue, discipline, and development stage before requesting an evaluation.
Ask for evidence-linked feedback that references exact sections, pages, or claims from the manuscript.
Use targeted follow-up reviews after revisions to check whether weak dimensions improved.
anti_patterns: Do not treat rubric scores as a substitute for domain expert judgment.
Do not request a comprehensive review without providing the manuscript or enough section detail.
Do not use the score calculator with sensitive files or output paths that should not be overwritten.

faq.question: What types of scholarly work can this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It supports research papers, proposals, literature reviews, thesis chapters, and conference abstracts.

faq.question: Does it provide numeric scores?
faq.answer: Yes. It can score applicable dimensions on a five-point scale and summarize aggregate quality.

faq.question: Can it review only one section?
faq.answer: Yes. You can request a targeted review of methodology, literature, writing, citations, or another dimension.

faq.question: Does it verify citations automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can assess citation quality from provided text, but it does not independently verify every source.

faq.question: Is the Python score calculator required?
faq.answer: No. The script is optional and only helps calculate aggregate scores from local JSON inputs.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish in the marketplace?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low residual risk from local file handling and external documentation links.
